schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: **Main physical differences (practical takeaways):** - **Local energy:** gyro comoving frame typically reads *higher* local electromagnetic energy near moving conductors / rotating dipole due to induced electric fields \(\mathbf E^{(u)}\approx\mathbf E^{(n)}+\mathbf v\times\mathbf B^{(n)}\). Expect \(\mathcal E^{(u)}>\mathcal E^{(n)}\) where \(\mathbf v\cdot\mathbf S^{(n)}\) is positive. - **Poynting flow / momentum:** directions differ — Poynting vector is Lorentz-shifted. In the rotating coordinate frame nonzero metric shift \(\beta^i\) produces apparent flux even for stationary fields there; these contributions may vanish or appear differently in the gyro frame. - **Principal stresses:** eigenvalues shift under boost. Tension along magnetic field lines remains (magnetic tension), but the projection onto the gyro’s structural axes changes — stresses that looked benign in the Eulerian frame can concentrate along a particular bearing or bolt in the gyro frame. - **Off-diagonals (shear):** Eulerian frame often shows larger shear components because of metric shift coupling; in a comoving orthonormal tetrad these can shrink but mechanical anisotropic stresses persist.
theory.technical_interpretation: Executive Summary (One Page) (2) is an inequality / bound, written mathcal E^(u)>mathcal E^(n). It states an inequality or bound that constrains the admissible range of the quantity. It is built from E, u, n. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
